messages.content: You are a helpful customer support assistant for TAIKAI, a hackathon and open innovation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely based on your knowledge of TAIKAI's products and services.
Where can I see the hackathons I can join?

To find hackathons to join, head over to /hackathons. There, you’ll see all available challenges in one place and can easily filter them by industry, such as Web3, AI, Health, Education, and more. You can also sort the list by Relevance or Most Recent, and search by keyword to quickly discover opportunities that match your interests.
You are a helpful customer support assistant for TAIKAI, a hackathon and open innovation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely based on your knowledge of TAIKAI's products and services.
Can't find a user when adding team members. Do they need to be in the same hackathon first?

If you can’t find someone when adding them to your team, it’s usually because they haven’t registered for the same hackathon yet. To appear in the search, the user must have completed registration for that challenge. Please have your teammate confirm their registration, and make sure the username is spelled correctly when you search for them.
You are a helpful customer support assistant for TAIKAI, a hackathon and open innovation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely based on your knowledge of TAIKAI's products and services.
What’s the difference between Solo tracks and Global tracks?
Voting tracks are the categories jury members vote in. Solo (Independent) tracks are voted on within a specific category, while Global tracks are voted on across all categories.
You are a helpful customer support assistant for TAIKAI, a hackathon and open innovation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely based on your knowledge of TAIKAI's products and services.
I enabled withdrawals but still can’t move my LX tokens. Any idea why?

You may not be able to withdraw $LX tokens if 2FA is disabled, your balance is insufficient, or the destination wallet address is invalid. Please enable 2FA in Settings > Account first, confirm you have enough balance, and verify that the wallet is a valid Polygon address.
You are a helpful customer support assistant for TAIKAI, a hackathon and open innovation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely based on your knowledge of TAIKAI's products and services.
How can I apply for a job or internship at TAIKAI?
Visit TAIKAI's official website for career opportunities, or send your application to support@taikai.network indicating you are interested in a role at TAIKAI.
You are a helpful customer support assistant for TAIKAI, a hackathon and open innovation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely based on your knowledge of TAIKAI's products and services.
The rules aren't clear to me. What are we supposed to turn in at each stage?

Submission requirements vary by hackathon. Check the Rules tab and each phase's description in the Timeline tab for details on what is expected at each stage. Contact the organizer if the requirements are unclear.

What is the difference between a "Challenge" and a "Hackathon"?
They are the same thing. TAIKAI uses these terms interchangeably -- a Challenge (also called Hackathon) is a competition hosted by an organization where participants submit projects.
You are a helpful customer support assistant for TAIKAI, a hackathon and open innovation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely based on your knowledge of TAIKAI's products and services.
What’s the process for adding a cover picture and profile image to a project?

To publish your project, first go to its edit page. In the project settings, you’ll find upload options for two images: the logo/avatar and the cover image. Upload both files, save your changes, and once that’s done, you can publish the project.

I keep getting TAIKAI promo emails. How do I turn them off?

Yes. Each marketing email includes an unsubscribe link at the bottom. Click it to opt out. Note that unsubscribing from marketing emails will not affect transactional emails (such as password resets or hackathon notifications) -- manage those separately in Settings > Notifications.

Can participants change their hackathon registration form answers (e.g., location preference) after submitting?

This depends on your hackathon settings. You can configure whether participants can edit their registration answers in the hackathon admin panel. If editing is disabled, participants must contact you as the organizer to request a change.

What should we submit at every phase of the hackathon?

Submission requirements vary by hackathon. Check the Rules tab and each phase's description in the Timeline tab for details on what is expected at each stage. Contact the organizer if the requirements are unclear.

I signed up for a hackathon but I'm getting a "Permission Denied" message. Why is that?

This can happen if the hackathon requires organizer approval of registrations and yours hasn't been approved yet, or if you are trying to access a section restricted to a different role. Contact the hackathon organizer to confirm your registration status.

The jury member has the email invitation, but when they log in there’s no voting option. Help?

If the jury member can’t vote after receiving an invitation, a few things may be causing it: they may not have clicked the confirmation link in the invitation email, the voting period may not have opened yet, or they may be assigned to a voting track that currently has no active projects. Ask them to look for the confirmation link in their email and verify that they are registered as a jury member on the challenge page.

Does TAIKAI handle prize disputes or is that on the organizer?

TAIKAI facilitates prize distribution via its platform token system. If an organizer doesn’t distribute the prizes, reach out to support@taikai.network and our team will look into it. That said, the organizer remains ultimately responsible for delivering the prize.

Can I host a hackathon on TAIKAI for free?
TAIKAI offers different plans for organizers. Contact support@taikai.network or visit the "Organize Your Hackathon" page to learn about free and paid options.

My Hackathons tab is showing all hackathons, not only my own. Is that normal?

The Hackathons tab on your profile only becomes visible once you have participated in 6 or more challenges. Before that threshold is reached, the tab may not appear or its display may vary. If you believe you are seeing someone else's hackathons on your profile, try clearing your browser cache and logging out and back in. Contact support if the problem continues.

I already signed up for the hackathon — can I update my answers somewhere?

Changing submitted registration answers, such as switching from online to in-person attendance, is only possible if the hackathon organizer has enabled that option. You can confirm this by checking the Preferences tab on the challenge page. If the fields are editable there, you can make the update yourself. If they’re not, the best next step is to contact the organizer and request the change.

I’m confused about the ranking section — what’s it for?

On the Ranking page (/ranking), you can view three different leaderboards: Participants, which are ranked based on project activity; Juries, which are ranked based on voting activity; and Global, which combines all activity into one ranking.

What tabs are on my profile?

Your profile has these tabs: Overview (bio, skills, experience, recent projects, POPs), Projects (all submitted projects, visible with 3+), Hackathons (challenges you participated in, visible with 6+), POPs (your Proof of Participation NFTs, visible with 5+), Followers/Following, and Transactions (token history).

My project needs a description to be published but I can't find where to enter it. Where is it?

The project description is in the project's edit page under the "Description" section (rich text editor). This is separate from the short teaser you entered when creating the project. Fill in the description there, save, and then publish.

What do we do if the person who owns the project can’t publish it before the deadline?

Project ownership is not transferable, so if only one team member can publish the project, it’s important to avoid leaving it until the last moment. We recommend publishing the project well before the deadline or coordinating in advance with your team owner. If you’re already close to the deadline and the owner is unavailable, please reach out to support@taikai.network as soon as possible.

Why is my position still showing on Matchmaking? How do I close it?

To manage who can apply to your team project, go to your project page and select the Positions tab. You can create positions there, and they are open by default. If you want to stop applications for a position, edit it and change its status to closed. Any closed position will no longer be visible on the Matchmaking tab.
You are a helpful customer support assistant for TAIKAI, a hackathon and open innovation platform. Answer questions accurately and concisely based on your knowledge of TAIKAI's products and services.
How are invites handled in TAIKAI? Can users accept or reject them from the dashboard?

Project owners manage invitations in the Participants tab, where they can directly invite participants to the project. After an invitation is sent, the invited user is notified and can review it in their Dashboard, where they have the option to accept or reject it.

I can’t find a card photo. Will the platform let me publish anyway?

A card image is recommended for better visibility, but it may not be required. Try publishing it—if an image is mandatory, the platform will show an error. If you need to publish urgently without one, contact TAIKAI support.

My account isn’t appearing anymore after I signed up with a social login. Do I need to use the same provider every time?

If you signed up using a social login, your TAIKAI account is connected to that specific provider, so you’ll need to use the same one you used originally. Please try each provider you may have used, such as Google, GitHub, LinkedIn, or MetaMask, to see if one of them brings up your account. If you still can’t find or access it, reach out to support@taikai.network and share the email address associated with your social account so the team can help you further.

Which wallets are supported by RainbowKit on TAIKAI?
TAIKAI uses RainbowKit for wallet connections, supporting MetaMask and any wallet compatible with the RainbowKit ecosystem (WalletConnect, Coinbase Wallet, etc.).

I get a chain/network error when using MetaMask in mobile Safari. Is this a known issue?

MetaMask on mobile Safari can have compatibility issues. Please try opening TAIKAI from the MetaMask in-app browser instead, or use a desktop browser with the MetaMask extension. WalletConnect is also a good mobile-friendly alternative.

There’s no Join button on the challenge page. Did registration close already?

If you don’t see a Join button, it’s usually because registration is not open yet, it has ended, the hackathon is private/invitation-only, or your account is already registered. You can confirm the registration period in the Timeline tab.

I think I connected my wallet correctly, but TAIKAI still shows it as disconnected. Do I need to reconnect or something?

After connecting your Ethereum wallet, please refresh the page and confirm that your wallet extension is unlocked. You should also verify that you approved the connection request in your wallet, since missing that step can cause the status to remain disconnected. If the problem continues, disconnect the wallet and connect it again. Should it still appear as disconnected afterward, please contact TAIKAI support for further help.

I’m not seeing a send option for LX. How do I transfer it to someone on TAIKAI?
Open the Transfer modal (accessible from balance displays and user profiles), enter the recipient's username and the amount. A 2FA code is required to confirm.

Why is there no option to withdraw from this challenge?

Please note that some challenges do not permit participants to withdraw after they have joined. If you want to leave the challenge, you should contact the hackathon organizer directly to ask about your options.

How do I fix the countdown/timeline shown in the hackathon? It’s confusing my users.

What the timeline shows is automatically determined by the start and end dates of each phase in your hackathon settings. So, if you want to adjust the displayed timeline—for example, the number of days remaining—you’ll need to update the phase dates in the admin panel. If you’re unsure how to do that or need help fine-tuning the display, TAIKAI support can assist you.

I can’t create an account because it says my email already exists, yet the reset link says the email is invalid. Is there another way to log in?

This issue can happen when the account wasn’t actually created with an email and password, but instead with a social login (Google, GitHub, LinkedIn) or an Ethereum wallet. In those cases, there is no password associated with the email, which is why the “forgot password” process reports the email as invalid even though the address already exists in our system. Please try signing in using the original login method you used to register. If you’re still unable to get in, contact our support team at support@taikai.network.

Which blockchain does TAIKAI use?
TAIKAI operates on the Polygon network for token transactions, deposits, and withdrawals.

I’m stuck on 2FA and my code isn’t working. What should I do?

Please double-check your email/username and password — passwords are case-sensitive. If your account is locked, wait until the lockout period ends. If it’s suspended, you’ll see a message with the details, and you should contact support. For 2FA problems, use a backup code. If you forgot your password, click “Can’t log in?” on the login page or go to /password/reset.

How do I join TAIKAI with a referral from a friend?
Yes — you can sign up using a friend’s referral link by visiting /signup/{friendUsername}. Once the referral program is fully active, your friend will receive Points for the referral.

How does the challenge timeline work from registration to results?

A challenge is divided into several phases, like Registration, Submission, Voting, and Results. Every phase has its own start and end dates, and the Timeline tab on the challenge page shows the complete schedule.

Can everyone see the live streams, or are they private?

Some stages in TAIKAI live streams are restricted, and access can depend on your role. For example, a stage may be open only to participants, only to jury members, or to both participants and jury members. If you try to open a stage without the proper access, the platform will redirect you to log in or display an access restriction notice.

What’s the utility of $LX tokens?
KAI ($LX or TKAI) is TAIKAI’s utility token, used for prize pools, jury voting/backing, and platform rewards.

I unsubscribed from TAIKAI emails and now I’m not seeing team invites. Can you resubscribe me?

To start receiving TAIKAI emails again, add support@taikai.network and no-reply@taikai.network to your safe senders/whitelist and make sure to check your spam or junk folder. If you unsubscribed from these messages, please contact support@taikai.network and ask to be resubscribed to transactional emails.

Please try each provider you may have used, such as Google, GitHub, LinkedIn, or MetaMask, to see if one of them brings up your account. If you still can’t find or access it, reach out to support@taikai.network and share the email address associated with your social account so the team can help you further.

Having trouble adding my X profile URL to my account. Should it be @name, name, or the full link?

Enter your Twitter/X handle without the @ symbol and without the full URL -- just your username (e.g. yourhandle). Some profile fields accept the full URL format (https://x.com/yourhandle); if one format gives an error, try the other.

Why am I not seeing new messages unless I reload the page?
Yes. Messages update in real-time via GraphQL subscriptions. Unread message counts are displayed as badges.

Which formats are supported for project attachments on TAIKAI?

TAIKAI supports common file formats for project attachments. For images (logo, cover), JPEG and PNG are recommended. For attachments, PDF and common document formats are generally accepted. Very large files may be rejected -- check the upload prompt for size limits.

How do I earn Points?

You can earn points through Profile Completion Quests (10 points each for uploading a photo, cover image, filling in your name, bio, job title, skills, academic experience, work experience, city, country, connecting a wallet, Twitter, GitHub, or LinkedIn) and Activity Quests (e.g. receiving $LX rewards from challenges at 50x multiplier, winning a challenge category for 50 points).

Need my invite/referral link ASAP — where is it located?
Your referral link is taikai.network/signup/{yourUsername}. You can share this link directly. You may also find a share option on the Points page (/points).

Continue on the password reset screen is still greyed out even after I type my email. Any idea why?

Make sure you've entered a valid email address in the field -- the button activates only when a properly formatted email is entered. If the button remains greyed out after entering your email, try a different browser or clear your cache, then contact support if the issue persists.

I'm confused about the token stuff—what transaction types are there exactly?
TAIKAI token transactions can be: allocate, fund, back, delegate, transfer, divmember, divbacker, withdraw, and deposit.

When a challenge supports live streaming, TAIKAI shows a "Stream" button on the challenge page. Simply click that button to be taken to the live stream page and start watching.

Why can't I see the voting results anywhere in the event page?
The Juries tab shows jury members by voting track. The Results tab (when available) shows final project rankings and reward distribution. The Transactions tab shows all backing transactions.

How can I earn points fast on the platform?

Points are earned in two main ways: Profile Completion Quests and Activity Quests. Profile quests give 10 points each for actions like adding your photo, cover image, name, bio, job title, skills, academic and work experience, city, country, and connecting a wallet, Twitter, GitHub, or LinkedIn. Activity quests can also reward you, for example by turning $LX rewards from challenges into points at a 50x multiplier, or by winning a challenge category for 50 points.

Can you tell me why my existing account won’t let me use Google sign-in?

If your account was created with email and password, Google or Microsoft may not be linked to it. Please log in with your email and password first, then connect a social account in Settings > Account > Connected Accounts. Also, Microsoft login isn’t currently supported, so use Google, GitHub, or LinkedIn instead.

Is the unread badge supposed to change automatically when I get a new message?

Absolutely. Messages on TAIKAI are delivered in real time via GraphQL subscriptions, which means updates appear instantly as they happen. In addition, unread message counts are displayed as badges so you can easily keep track of new activity.

Can I remove myself from the marketing mailing list?

Yes. Each marketing email includes an unsubscribe link at the bottom. Click it to opt out. Note that unsubscribing from marketing emails will not affect transactional emails (such as password resets or hackathon notifications) -- manage those separately in Settings > Notifications.

Usernames and passwords requirements please?
Usernames must be 2-39 characters, alphanumeric and hyphens only, and cannot start or end with a hyphen. Passwords must be between 9 and 72 characters. Full names must be 2-150 characters.

I think I changed my account email and forgot to confirm it. Now I’m completely unable to log in. How can I recover my account?

If you changed your email address and can’t log in anymore, the issue is usually that the new email still needs to be confirmed. TAIKAI sends a confirmation email to the new address, and you must click the link inside it before that email becomes active for login. Check the new mailbox carefully, including the spam or junk folder. If you’ve lost access to both your old and new email addresses, please contact support@taikai.network so the team can assist you.

How do I manage active logins? I'm worried someone else may still be signed in.
Go to Settings > Sessions to see your active login sessions, and revoke any session to log out a specific device or browser.

I can’t find the import invite feature. How do I use a CSV file to invite users?

The CSV invite feature is meant for organizers and can be found in the hackathon management tools. Participants who want to add teammates should use the regular individual invite flow from the Participants tab instead. If you're organizing the hackathon and don't see the option, look for "Import" or "Invite via CSV" in the admin panel, or reach out to TAIKAI support for assistance.

I'm new here—how do I find teammates?

Multiple ways: (1) Browse open positions on the challenge's Matchmaking tab; (2) View all registered participants on the Participants tab -- some may have a "Looking for team" badge; (3) Create open positions on your project to attract applicants.

What is EIP-4361 and why is it asking me to sign a message?

How long does blockchain confirmation usually take for a POP mint?

After you click “Mint POP,” the NFT is processed on the blockchain and will first show as MINTING before changing to MINTED. If the POP is still in MINTING after 24 hours, please contact TAIKAI support for help.

How do I get the 10 points for profile quests?

Points are earned in two main ways: Profile Completion Quests and Activity Quests. TAIKAI’s voting/judging system is designed around a voting cart for jury members. To participate, you must register as a Jury member. Once voting opens, you can browse the projects submitted to the hackathon or challenge, add the ones you want to evaluate to your voting cart, and include the required scores and assessments. If the event uses weighted criteria, you’ll also need to provide an appraisal for each criterion. After you’ve finished reviewing, simply check out your cart, which submits all of your votes at once.

This issue can happen when the account wasn’t actually created with an email and password, but instead with a social login (Google, GitHub, LinkedIn) or an Ethereum wallet. In those cases, there is no password associated with the email, which is why the “forgot password” process reports the email as invalid even though the address already exists in our system. Please try signing in using the original login method you used to register. If you’re still unable to get in, contact our support team at support@taikai.network.

What kinds of token transactions are there on TAIKAI?

Token transactions on TAIKAI include several types: allocate for prize pool allocation, fund for supporting a challenge, back for jury voting or backing, delegate for passing voting power to someone else, transfer for user-to-user transfers, divmember for rewards to project team members, divbacker for rewards to jury members, withdraw for sending tokens to an external Polygon wallet, and deposit for bringing tokens in from an external Polygon wallet.

What’s the best way to get points?

Points are earned in two main ways: Profile Completion Quests and Activity Quests. Profile quests give 10 points each for actions like adding your photo, cover image, name, bio, job title, skills, academic and work experience, city, country, and connecting a wallet, Twitter, GitHub, or LinkedIn. Activity quests can also reward you, for example by turning $LX rewards from challenges into points at a 50x multiplier, or by winning a challenge category for 50 points.

How do I edit my account profile on TAIKAI?
Go to your profile page, click Edit, make your changes to your avatar, cover image, bio, skills, experience, and social links, then click Save.

Where is the CSV invite option for my hackathon?

The CSV invite feature is meant for organizers and can be found in the hackathon management tools. Participants who want to add teammates should use the regular individual invite flow from the Participants tab instead. If you're organizing the hackathon and don't see the option, look for "Import" or "Invite via CSV" in the admin panel, or reach out to TAIKAI support for assistance.

My submission is locked and I can’t edit it. Did the deadline pass?

If you’re unable to edit your submission, the most common reason is that the challenge has already reached its deadline or final validation, which disables editing. Please confirm that the challenge is still active, since projects cannot be modified after the deadline. Also double-check your submission details: the project name must be 3–50 characters and the teaser must be 3–200 characters. If either field is outside those limits, editing may not work properly.
